research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
case_title: Delayed metastatic cancer diagnosis after repeated back-pain presentations
initial_situation: Patient with prior breast cancer history developed severe back pain.
what_happened: Multiple contacts occurred across ED, GP, nurse, physiotherapy and out-of-hours care; diagnosis came after repeated presentations.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Red flags and repeated symptom pattern were not connected early enough.
lesson_or_pattern: Continuity and IT systems must surface repeated unresolved symptoms and cancer history.
product_or_system_implication: Same-symptom clustering, unresolved-problem tracking, named follow-up owner

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
case_title: Missed bowel lesion on scan with delayed diagnosis
initial_situation: Patient had imaging that contained an abnormality.
what_happened: Abnormality was not identified and later follow-up was delayed.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Scan finding missed and result follow-up failed.
lesson_or_pattern: Imaging workflows need closed-loop review and patient-visible status.
product_or_system_implication: Closed-loop scan tracker with escalation for unreviewed abnormal results

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
case_title: Sepsis recognition failures repeated across NHS complaints
initial_situation: Patients presented with signs that should have triggered sepsis recognition/escalation.
what_happened: Delayed diagnosis/treatment and communication/follow-up failures recurred across cases.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Sepsis red flags and family concerns were not acted on quickly enough.
delay_or_timeline: hours to fatal deterioration in some examples
outcome_if_known: death or severe harm in complaint examples
lesson_or_pattern: Emergency escalation must include family concern and deterioration tracking.
product_or_system_implication: Family concern escalation field and sepsis reassessment prompts

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
case_title: Bedouin community access and continuity barriers
initial_situation: Communities in recognized and unrecognized villages seek care through formal and sometimes out-of-system routes.
what_happened: Physical access, health-literacy barriers, and out-of-system care complicate care continuity.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Signals can be missed when care occurs outside the formal Israeli record system.
lesson_or_pattern: Integrated records alone are insufficient for marginalized periphery communities.
product_or_system_implication: Capture out-of-system visits and language/cultural access needs

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
initial_situation: People with TB symptoms often first consult traditional healers or AYUSH/non-allopathic providers.
what_happened: Intervention linked these providers to referral and free chest x-ray.
what_was_missed_or_identified: TB identification improved; diagnostic delay reduced.
lesson_or_pattern: Informal providers can be part of safer identification if connected to formal diagnostics.
product_or_system_implication: Referral tools and diagnostic vouchers for informal/community providers

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
initial_situation: Patients developed symptoms and moved through multiple facilities as symptoms worsened.
what_happened: Diagnostic capacity such as imaging/pathology was concentrated in higher-level centers and patients often self-referred.
lesson_or_pattern: Referral completion, funds, transport, and diagnostic capacity determine identification.

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
case_title: WHO RAcE/iCCM CHW childhood illness workflow
condition_or_issue: malaria, pneumonia, diarrhea in children under 5
initial_situation: Children with acute illness were assessed near home by trained CHWs.
what_happened: CHWs diagnosed, treated, and referred according to iCCM protocols.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Danger signs and treatable childhood illnesses identified closer to community.
lesson_or_pattern: CHWs can be first-assessment nodes but require protocols, supplies, and referral loops.
product_or_system_implication: Offline CHW protocols, stock status, and referral confirmation

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
case_title: Brian Sinclair emergency department waiting-room death
initial_situation: Indigenous patient arrived with a referral note.
what_happened: He waited 34 hours in the ED; staff reportedly assumed intoxication.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Clinical need and referral note were not acted on.
lesson_or_pattern: Racism and social triage can block basic assessment and escalation.
product_or_system_implication: Anti-bias triage audit, Indigenous liaison escalation, did-not-wait prevention

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
condition_or_issue: ED presentations by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander patients
initial_situation: ED sought to improve identification and culturally safe treatment of Indigenous patients.
what_happened: Project focused on culturally and clinically appropriate care and reducing did-not-wait outcomes.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Risk of leaving before diagnostic workup was targeted.
lesson_or_pattern: Cultural safety must be operationalized in triage and ED flow.
product_or_system_implication: Real-time liaison routing and cultural-safety workflow

research_topic: Research E — Global, cultural, and resource-level comparison of health issue identification workflows
condition_or_issue: hepatitis C, diabetes risk, hypercholesterolemia, immunization gaps
initial_situation: Migrant patients had incomplete routine screening and missing immunization records.
what_was_missed_or_identified: New conditions were identified and vaccination gaps surfaced.
outcome_if_known: 12 new conditions found among 99 screened migrants
lesson_or_pattern: Targeted intake tools can identify hidden conditions when prior records are missing.
product_or_system_implication: Migrant-specific EHR screening and vaccination reconstruction
